title: if you must..
text:
Not impressed at all.. I'm on my second pair of Apple Airpods and have used them almost exclusively for the gym. Sadly after about 14 months my first pair was shot. It was likely due to sweat "rusting" the charging rings. Not willing to see what Apple could do I went ahead and purchased a second pair, this time careful not to throw them back in the charging dock immediately after each workout. Well after about six months now I've started to experience the same issue I had with my first pair... Which lead me to bite the bullet and pay through the nose to get these water-resistant Beats wireless headphones...

As soon as I fired up Spotify I could not believe how bland the sound was.. Wow, paying $100 more for these I foolishly believed the Beats moniker would at least have a slight upgrade over the Airpods but nope, not to my ears..Listening to Podcasts sounded exactly like Airpods too.... All that said, I realize I'm a slave to Apple so am willing to pay the $$$ for what is o

title: The apex of gym/workout buds!!!
text: First off, I'm a bit of an audiophile and own many sets of earbuds/headphones/stereo systems, but the beats pro buds are one set that I have kept thru everything. Mostly for their superior ear fit and exceptional staying power during workouts, outdoor activities and basically any event that requires me to move alot but allows them. The beats pros may not be the most impressive sounding buds I own but they are the most complete package all around. They come with multiple ear tips for a good fit, (I have small ears), and hold an impressive charge even without the comically large case that holds a charge itself to recharge your buds when your on the go. They feel good and wrap around the ear in a way that feels secure without being annoying or bothersome. The sound, as I've stated is not the pinnacle of wireless sound but easily stands among the best and when combined with the practicallity of their brilliant form factor, add up to be the best all around wireless earbuds for those on the

title: Disappointing
text:
I had high expectations for the Powerbeats Pro, and have ended up highly disappointed.

Fit
If there's one good thing I can say about the Powerbeats Pro, is that they don't fall off like AirPods, EarPods and most wireless and wired ear buds do. The Powerbeats Pro come with four pairs of ear tips in different sizes. Too bad none of them actually fit my ears. I wish Apple would just make their current AirPods with an ear hook to prevent them from falling off our ears. Anyway, the ear tips included with the Powerbeats resulted in either flat out pain or discomfort. After trying out all four pairs of ear tips, I ended up going back to the medium ones that come installed out of the box.

Sound
Sound quality in ear buds is directly related to their fit and how well that fit can create a seal. Needless to say that I cannot get good sound with the ear tips that don't cause me pain, and I can't get good sound quality for too long with the painful ear tips. The end

title: Great Bluetooth earphones
text: This is the 2nd pair of these that I have had.  The first pair lasted me about 3 years.  I loved them because they hook over the ear and I’m one of those people that no matter what I buy, they won’t stay in my ear. So the hook for me is perfect.  I used the first pair day in and day out with out any complaints until they stopped holding a charge and I couldn’t get more than 45-60 minutes out of them.  I figured it was time for an upgrade so I bought the Beats Pro fit. The sound from those was infinitely better and the noise cancellation was awesome.  But they kept falling out of my ears.  So I went back to power Beats Pro.  Now I’m on my 2nd pair and couldn’t be happier.  Well I could be happier if they sounded as good as the beats fit pro and had the same noise cancellation.  Besides that I absolutely love my Power Beast Pro.

title: Hurts ears!
text:
First off, they sound great (no noise canceling though) and fit snug but after 20+ minutes of use, they begin to hurt my ears. It's the "hooks" that hurt my ears. Seems that the hook material used is way too hard and positioned where it really grasps your ear tight. The best i could describe the pain is a "really deep ache" behind your ear (where the hook rests). If you don't touch your ear, you won't notice any pain but once you go to fiddle with the headphones or take them off, you'll feel the pain and it takes a solid 10 minutes for it to go away after taking them off. This is strange because Im coming from having the Powerbeats 3 and the hooks on those NEVER bothered my ears.

Really disappointed because I was looking forward to using these for my workouts. Going to return them in the morning.

title: Fits over glasses frames, stays put when running
text:
I wear glasses, and these fit comfortably over my ears and frames. I don’t know how they seat and block out sound for non-glasses people, but I still have awareness of surrounding noises, which I consider a good thing. A lot of earbuds get uncomfortable fast, but the buds on these do not press heavily on my ear canals and my ears do not fatigue wearing them.

They don’t come loose when running for a few hours, and allow enough noise in that I am able to hear traffic and be environmentally aware.

Battery life seems good. I used them constantly on a 4 hour 30 minute run, and they did not die (audiobook, not music, not sure if that makes a difference for battery drain).

The on-ear controls are fantastic. I don’t like touch controls, but these are actual buttons, and are simple to operate: button on the ear pauses/unpauses, rocker button on the top of each earpiece controls volume. I haven’t used them for any calls, so I can’t say how they work on that front

title: Ok, for Android
text:
I bought these last December and use them 8 hours daily at work (mostly podcasts). Battery still lasts about 8 hours, which is notable.
These pair much better with iPhone, interaction with siri, etc. With my android, it disconnects about every 4 hours (for no apparent reason) and I have to repair it. Not a huge deal, but a little annoying. Never did try the Beats App on the playstore, but lots of bad reviews there.
They rest nicely in the ears...I often forget which ear it's in if I have to press the pause button. Never had one fall out, and a little light rain is no problem for them.
A little expensive, yes...but, for me, well worth the cost.

title: Amazon Renewed and got Screwed
text:
[[VIDEOID:b779bafe29b2fdc9c08e29ebc78a4a6b]] These sound amazing I'm sure - just not the defective pair I bought through Amazon renew. They were still close to $200. When I paired them and went for a run I wasn't that impressed. They sounded good - not great. Two months in, I'm out on a run and the left bud suddenly glitches and for the next few minutes I experience what non defective beats sound like - Incredible! I've had to "forget device" and re-pair them several times because the left one will not work sometimes when I put them in. The battery life is great - I work 11 hour shifts Monday - Friday and use them to communicate with Dispatch or listen to music and they last all day. They were returned and registered under someone else’s name when I bought them and I should’ve sent them back. Will probably buy another pair eventually but not through Renew.

Update:
A few months in, a full charge lasts about 6 hours which isn't bad , they still charge quickly. The sound

title: Multiple issues, not balanced both sides
text:
I am big fan of Powerbeats and ordered Pro on very first day because of the style and convenient during my run. However it has multiple issues which has to be identified before release.

1.Both ears not synced multiple times. One earphone has delayed sync. I am facing this issue at least once in an hour of usage.
2.When you take out one side of Earphones, the sound is paused and need to play again.
3.lip sync delayed sometimes while watching streaming.

All these issues expected and accepted for $25 worth of earphones not from $250 - Beats Pro Earphones .

title: Long term review Charging/Conectivity
text: I'm giving a 4 star but would give 3.5 if I could. Sound quality is ok not great, they stay in place and battery life is very good. Biggest issue I have had is one earphone dropping bluetooth conection or both disconnecting. Second issue has been charging which many here have complained about. After charging complete and then finding out that only one earphone charged and one is dead. I have found a solution for some unexplained reason if I charge while the power case is plugged in sometimes only one will charge but if I unplug the power case both will charge completly.

title: Great when they work, completely useless when they don't
text: I bought these a few months ago when they were on sale. They're great when they decide to co-operate. However, most times, only one of the headphones will connect and the other is simply silent. Then, when you want to pair them with another device besides your phone, they fail to go into discover mode. The only way to get them to go into discover mode is to reset the headphones, which means your previous pairing is also no longer going to work. This gets you into an endless loop of resetting, and pairing, resetting, and pairing. It's a mess. Another issue is the charging case... sometimes the headphones charge, sometimes only one charges, and some times the case goes completely dead overnight because it sat in some sort of "active" mode for no reason at all. Bottom line, these headphones are plagued with bugs. I recommend going with another, more well-established player in the blue tooth headset business.

title: Hard To Find A Good Fit. I Tried. A Lot.
text:
My husband has a set of these and really likes them. I should have known better. He’s not a music lover and prefers to wear one at a time. Old school. Plus longer battery life.
The main reason I tried them is they’re well reviewed for exercise and I use a yoga swing. If anything could hang on while you’re upside down it seemed like these should. If that’s all I needed them for, I’d still have sent them back.
For me, I found them painful to wear for any length of time. I couldn’t find a way to solve it. As for the tips, the problem seemed to be the angle. Then there are the loops, and that was more about finding a way to adjust them so they didn’t make my earlobes sore. I never could wear them for more than an hour without my ears aching.
Plus, since they’re so bulky, wearing both at the same time made me feel like a critter out of Star Trek.
The battery life was okay, but not great. They did recharge quickly. On the whole the battery is better than the Jabra 75t Act

title: Same sound as Airpods
text:
These are Airpods in a diferent casing; absolutley no sound difference at all.

The only reason to buy these is, If you don't care about $100 and don't have airpods.

title: Having a problem with these headphones
text:
I just got these today. I noticed that some people are complaining about these headphone on here. I’m glad I didn’t listen. These are ok. Not a problem. Highly recommend!

Update: 05 November 2019 - I’m having a problem with these headphones. I haven’t been able to use them yet at all during my workout at the gym. Everytime I try to connect them, I’m only getting 1 side turned on. Either the left OR the right, but not both at the same time. It’s only happening at the gym. When I come back home and try it, it works fine. I’m also having a problem with charging these up. Only one side is fully charging. The other side is NOT fully charging. But when I put it in the charger, the light goes out, indicating that both are fully charged. But they’re not! If I continue to have problems with these headphones, they’re going back for a refund. Don’t need headphone drama and disappointment.

Sent them back for a refund. These headphones were nothing but drama. Didn’t work out

title: Best workout earphones ever
text: If you work a lot these are a must have. They won’t move at all on your ears and the sound is amazing. The bass on them are really nice too.

title: Avoid the reviews, buy the product
text:
Reviewing after clocking 40 miles, 30 days of use.

I spent hours searching for a earpiece which won’t keep falling off my ear during my outdoor and treadmill runs. I tend to focus on listening to my body while long distance running but felt the need for music during my speed workouts, purely for motivation.

After reading the bad reviews, I was doubtful if this Device will meet my requirements but then I decided to give it a try and not get influenced by the bad reviewers.

The battery lasts long enough and you don’t need to charge often. One full charge gives 2 weeks of full use.

The device doesn’t slide into the ear and the video clip on detail page is misleading. It does take a bit of manual adjustment to tuck it nicely in your ears but once it does, it stays there. Speed workouts or long distance, I never had any issues with the fit. You also get different soft buds to perfect it with your ear hole.

The volume buttons on the devi

title: Poor sound quality  - Not mask friendly
text:
Update: I received my Bose QC Noise Cancelling earbuds and right away I was in awe of the sound quality compared to the Powerbeats Pro. I don't mean to trash on Apple, but not sure why anyone would want any Apple H1 Chip earbuds including Airpods Pro other than for looks.

I really wanted to like these. I like the way they look and fit and I really like the iOS integration and automatic reading of text messages. But to be honest the sound quality is very tinny, flimsy with no depth and will almost always come flying off or get tangled when I take off my mask. My comparison is with the Bose Soundsport Free which I had been using for almost 2 years until I lost the right earbud recently. That had a much richer sound and a smaller profile and no hooks to get caught on.

I was thinking of the Airpod Pro but it uses the same sound chip as these Powerbeats, so I don't have high expectations for that either.

I hate leaving bad reviews but I think these powerbeat

title: My experience
text:
I will be totally honest, these things gave me a really hard time out of the box with pairing. I spent about 2 hours trying to figure out how they work. I even started contacting customer support within the first 20 minutes of unboxing. Every time I would go to pair, it would connect, until I went to play music, it would drop. No idea why it did that.

THAT being said...

I have had no issues with these at all since. I pair them to my fire TV, my MacBook Pro, my iPhone, as well as my Mighty Vibe that I use for workouts (check out my review for that, it's like a wireless iPod shuffle). Some people I read on here talk about the music quality being poor, but I guess I'm not from LA and work in a music studio, so I thin the sound is what I would hope for from any of my music devices. If you are worried at all about these falling out of your ears (I also had trauma from the skull candies before I decided to spend big boy amounts of money on my earphones, which would cons

title: Great - when they work. Right one refuses to charge.
text:
Another update: I’ve had two pairs of these now, for reasons that aren’t important, and they both have the same issue: the Right side refuses to charge. I will have them plugged in all night and then the right one is at 2%, and the left one at 100%. I’ve tried reseated, resetting, and cleaning terminals…nothing works. I have to wiggle it in there, sometimes that works. It’s frustrating because I’m nearly deaf in. My LEFT ear, so I really need the right one to work! Took away another star.

Update: These seem to want to work again. Perhaps it was a firmware update on Apple's end, but they're working. I'll give them a couple more stars, but not 5, because of the inconsistency.

Previous review
I kept these in a drawer to be used for telemeetings. about a year in, they started having connection problems. I went online, carefully cleaned the connections with alcohol, and they seemed to work fine again. But the connection problems became more frequent, and today

title: Garbage
text:
They are the absolute worst. Biggest problem is that, while they'll pair with your phone after some fiddling, they are unreliable - the left and right buds frequently lose pairing with each other. When this happens it creates strange sounds and gaps and means you have to stop what you're doing to try to get them paired again. Try doing that in the rain when you're out running. So I gave up using them in both ears, meaning they're not at all suitable for music. I had let the return period run out, so I'm stuck with them, so now I only use them one at a time and only for phone calls and podcasts, although even for that, the max volume is variable and is often not adequate. The microphone doesn't work consistently either. Batteries don't last long on a full charge after the first month or so (maybe 30 minutes). Sound is only so-so when they do work.

Crappy workmanship - one of the earbuds on mine falls apart repeatedly, and the silicone seal doesn't seat right so it's for s

title: Poor sound quality.
text:
High quality construction but absolutely TERRIBLE sound quality. Every thing I’ve read online about these say they are great. Makes me question all the “independent “ reviews I read.
It’s very disappointing because these have no bass or low mid Range response at all. I have $19 generic Walmart(wired) earbuds that blow these out of water in regards to sound quality. They only sound good with voices like a phone call, a pod cast or the voice in song. But for music you are screwed. I have to keep them because I need ear buds that stay put for my mountain bike rides and gym workouts. If sound quality doesn’t matter then these are a must have because the design is pretty much flawless. Great pairing, comfortable, well thought out design and looks good. I like my music to pump me up when I work out. With these that cannot happen.

title: Overrated - underperforms
text:
Great deal from Amazon - less than $150 new!
I do like the way they really hang on the ear - bit of a fiddle though to get them in; however once in they stay secure…but rather bulky.
Sound quality is very average - more like a $30 pair.
No bells and whistles like the AirPods Pro which is now priced in the same range.
The killer for me was the phone quality - people complained I sounded ‘muffled’. Not good for business.
So off they go back to Amazon. Disappointed but I’ll stay with the AirPods Pro.

title: Great
text: These work great!  Finally found earbuds that stay in place!  And the quality is great. I’m happy and would recommend these to anyone who struggle with the earbuds.

title: Not worth the price
text: These are good earphones but they're not $200 good.  I bought them because I have a fit issue with most earphones so I need the overear hook to keep the buds in place.  That part works well.  And once I got the right sized silicone tips on, the fit was pretty decent.  But I didn't like the controls (any time I had to re-seat the buds, I'd invariably hit the pause or change the volume, there just isn't a way to touch them without pushing a button) and the sound quality is OK but not amazing for my novice ear.  In the end, I returned them and went with a $30 pair that sounds fine and fits about the same.

title: Powerbeats Pro from a Android User
text: My first impressions of the Powerbeats Pro are positive. They have great sound, loud enough, and fairly connect quick to my Samsung Galaxy Note 8. I did install the Beats App and AirBattery from the Play Store; so I could check for updates and the battery levels. I'm surprised that I had no issues, considering my phone isn't an iPhone. I can't wait to try them on my iPad mini, if I can pry it from my wife's hands. Overall a great purchase.

title: Stays on Ears
text: I always have trouble with earbuds staying in my ears. These are great.  They stay with no problem and the sound is great!

title: Overall… Very happy
text: I first bought the Bose Noise cancelling earbuds and they were awful. They kept falling out so I couldn’t even tell if they had good sound quality or not. Also, trying to pair them to my devices was a nightmare and the controls were difficult to use. I returned them and got the Beats Prob earbuds. What took me days and days to figure out with Bose (and their support team)……it took me minute with Beats Pros. That is to say, they were extremely easy to put on. They stayed in the ears, had good sound, easy controls  and were ridiculously easy to pair with my Apple devices. I was over the moon happy.

title: To much treble
text: Everything about this product is great except the sound quality.  I have lost some of my hearing at the high end and these headphones actually bother my ears when I listen to music.  My cheap earbuds don't have as much treble and they dont bother me when listening to music.  Everything else about these earbuds was great including fit, phone call sound quality, and use while exercising.  I had to return them.

title: I'm not impressed. At all.
text: These were given to me as a gift brand new but I expected better from Apple (I know, wishful thinking). This headset costs $250 and at the most basic of functions, the case cannot charge wirelessly.  You look at any of these cheapy $50 pair of headphones low and behold, they have that functionality.  Secondly, I've only had them for about a month and a half with VERY LIGHT usage and already I have the hissing/buzzing noise in one of them to which that bud stopped working. This is a known issue. Lastly (because honestly, I could go on and on), these reeeeeealy don't sound that great in comparison to lower priced options. A premium feel with budget build. It's almost like putting a ford pinto engine in a corvette shell.

title: Poor functionality
text: No off button. They will stay on and die if you don't put them in the case. Even then sometimes they won't turn off. Sometimes only one dies early. Poor instructions for using. For the most popular headphones in the world they sure didn't put much time into ease of use and instruction, lamest thing I've ever seen. Try the generic ones for 40 dollars. Same sound quality anyway.

title: Problems - even with my second pair.  Don't bother
text:
Updates Nov16 - still hate these things pairing with my iPhone and other devices is nightmare. Having to carry around this insanely huge case also sucks. No returning for my third pair as one dies. Avoid!
—-
I am on my second pair. first pair stopped pairing with my iPhone. Guy in Apple Store finally agreed they were defective. Gave me a new set, worked for 2 weeks (warrantee period of course) not i have to repair them every few days. Hate having to drag aroound that huge case as if they become unpaired, its nearly impossible to get them paired again without that case. The opening of the case to pair is not a feature, but a major inconvenience. works inconsistently and requires you to have that massive case with you. Sound is just ok,not great. Pass on these

title: Meh
text:
I had a feeling these weren't going to be any good because my impression of Beats products is negative but I wanted something designed for activity that I could use while working outside. These need to be in the case to create a bluetooth connection. The case has rounded sides to it and the button you need to hold down for 5 seconds to connect is out near the edge. Additionally the curved additions to the body of the ear buds are right over the button. The buds don't connect via Bluetooth reliably so you end up pushing the the button while the case is rocking around with the buds in your way the whole time...total cluster. I returned these because eventually they wouldn't connect at all and replaced them with a pare of Bose buds that connect the second you take them out if the case and put them in your ears. Way better.

If you are into frustration and time wasting get these.

title: Finally a comfortable earbud that does not fall out!!!
text: I bought the Apple earpods but they kept falling out of my ears.  I guess my ear canal is too small.  So this over the ear earpod was recommended to me.  It is super comfortable and i can even lie down with it in my ear and be comfortable.  I did get the white color because it is easier to find them if they fall out.  My only comment is that the sound is not quite as great as over the ear headphones but perhaps that is a comment about all earpods.  I cannot imagine anyone not liking them.

title: These are by far the best headphones I've ever owned
text:
Before I start, the people with problems need to read the manual or watch a video on YouTube to see how they work. The YouTube video I watched answered every problem in the bad reviews.
The one bad thing is, I try to at turn turn on for work and only one works. If I have time to check the problem and fix it, it's no big deal. But whey I'm barely on time and don't have time to play with my headphones, it gets frustrating. On break, when I have time, they fix pretty easy, it's between 3 things. Even with that, they're still the best headphones I've ever owned.

These are great! My other Bluetooth headphones didn't sound the same out of both ears, made me think I was going deaf in my right ear! Not true! These sound prefect! Then they can work independently, one at a time, so at work when I have to deal with customers, I can have one in and one off so it just looks like a radio in my ear, like the walky talky radios. They also have a cool feature that if you roll them forw

title: Finally!!  The Earbuds I've Been Waiting For!
text: I've never tried airpods, but I have tried the earphones that Apple supplies you with their phones.  They DO NOT stay in my ear.  Knowing this, I've tried the PowerBeats 3.  They stay ON my ear because of the hooks, but that doesn't mean they stay IN my ear.  I've always had to push the earbuds deep into my ear canals no matter which soft tip I used.  Because of this, I went with the Studio3's.  Great sound.  Comfortable.  But they made my ears sweaty at the gym and they pushed my hair down on my daily commute (i know, first world problems).  Once I saw these, I wanted to give them a shot and lemme tell you, they do not disappoint.  They're light, they sound great, and best of all, they fit in my ear without me having to fidget with them after a few steps.  People complained the case was too big, but i actually like the chunkiness to the case.  I love that the case charges the Powerbeat Pros.  I love the way the earbuds snap right in with the strong magnetic connection.  They are $250,

title: If not a tech these are very confusing!
text: Had a hell of a time adjusting to my ears so l really never got to enjoy sound quality!

title: Nice working out ear phone.
text:
Cons.
uncomfortable on the back of the ear. And its bulky.

Pros
Good quality sound.
Long lasting battery life
Good quality build
It doesn't fall even intense work out.

I would still use it and i will still recommend it.

title: Awesome sound!!
text: I was struggling with what earbuds to purchase. I have first generation AirPods and I am disabled and was having problems putting them in my ears without dropping them. So, I was going back-and-forth in my head do I want the beats or the airpro. What helped me in my choice is the battery life. I'm use to around four hours of use before a charge is needed and I wanted some thing longer lasting. The Powerbeats pro met the expectation and the sound is awesome.

title: Good, not great - updated
text:
These are ok. The sound quality is good, not great. Probably as good as you can expect from true wireless earbuds. My biggest complaint is that you cannot tell if the earbuds are charging. My left earbud will not charge unless it is seated just so. I wiggle it into position, but cannot tell if it is charging or not until the next time I go to use it. 50% of the time my left earbud is dead and I have to put it back in the case and hope for the best next time I want to use it. I should have returned them right away.

Updated - after three months I would recommend you stay away from these. When I first purchased these I had intermittent connection or charging issues with the left earbud. Now the right one only connects 50% of the time. I am using these in an office environment, so they are not really subject to abuse, just going in and out of the case on a daily basis. If I want to use these for a Zoom meeting, I have to pop them out of the case 5 minutes early to make sure they

title: Best wireless headphones
text: Best wireless headphones I’ve ever had. After purchasing 2 apple AirPods (both stopped to work after a couple of years) I decided to switch to a different brand. I know this is technically an Apple product but it works amazing!

title: In love with them!
text: I was so happy to get these for a deal! These are the earphones I wanted! The battery lasts a long time and the charging case does too. They stay in the ear no matter what. They have the technology to stop the music or whatever you’re  watching, when you take out an earbud. Also, it shows you the battery life of each earbud if you’re only using one (ex. I only use the right earbud sometimes to hear my surroundings when doing something). I love them and I got them in the navy blue ones! I love the color! They also come with different ear cushions for different sized ears.

title: don't buy them if using with Windows PC
text: works great with iPhone but very problematic with Windows 10 PC. They worked most of time but having a call through Zoom and Teams, the mic function was freaking out and making noise. after trying so many times, I ended up filing a return. when it works, it works great, then when its mic is not working, it's the worst. I have been using Bose Q35 for years and never had that problem. could be perfect for iOS devices, though.

title: So very disappointed
text: The amount of issues I have had with these things is ridiculous for the brand and amount of money I spent. First of all RARELY do both eat buds connect the first time you get them out of the case. You have to usually put one back in for a minute then take it back out again to make it work. Secondly, it will randomly disconnect from my phone (that is less than feet away from my ears) to announce workouts and what not. I have went to setting and turned off everything I can, I have googled this issue to death, Apple basically says too bad so sad. Also I have had them for about 6 weeks now and the sound quality has dropped significantly. Sounds like there is a very slight static and the noises are at a distance even all the way turned up. My JLabs were a tenth of the price and worked just as good with a lot less issues. I was so excited for these but extremely disappointed. I’ll continue to use them since the return window has closed and I paid so much for them. But will NOT buy any beats

title: Sounds great
text: Fit better than any I have tried. Sound is great.

title: Good earbuds for working out
text: These are great because they stay very secure when doing rigorous activities. I wish they were a little more comfortable and noise canceling but overall great product!

title: Great headphones, awesome for working out
text: Solid noise cancellation, not 100% free of outside noise but does enough. Great connectivity, no complaints thus far

title: Over priced, No Noise Cancellation, What A Joke!
text: Overpriced and height headphones. No active noise cancellation. Incredibly overpriced

title: Distorted sound when talking to others
text: Buyer beware - I absolutely love to style, look and feel of these headphones. Unfortunately I am returning them because everyone I talk to has trouble hearing me through these headphones. Everyone says I sound distorted and unclear. I have the previous generation of Powerbeats and never had this issue. I tried updating settings and everything but nothing worked. They may be great to listen to music but are unfortunately bad for speaking to others on the phone.

title: Worked great for about a month
text: I purchased two of these for myself and my husband to use at the gym.  They worked well for about a month but both are now having problems charging.  Even placed in the case and plugged in the will either be dead on arrival or only last about 15 minutes.  It’s very disappointing for such an expensive headset.  Besides the charging issue when they do work they are amazing!

title: not for me
text: i really wish these fit better. they’re just awkward with my glasses.

title: Best gym headphones I’ve owned
text: These are awesome great sound great quality very crisp. noise cancellation is much better than I had expected it to be also drowns out all sound of the weights around me. very snug no issues at all running or training they stay very well in my ears and last about a week with just use during my workouts “1-2 hours daily”

title: Very comfortable on my ear and sound is great.
text: I got the blue beats pro ear buds.  They were recommended to from my coworkers.  These fit nicely and securely around my ears.  Sound is clear and I can walk away from my phone a good distance and still hear.  I got them to listen to music and my audiobooks.  The only thing I’m not really happy about is with noise canceling but I can’t seem to seem to ge that function to work.  I still can hear all my normal surroundings.  I highly recommend these.

title: Boomy bass is a thing of the past great all around sound
text: Overall excellent sound and have no problems with connectivity.

title: Best wireless headphone since Aretha Franklin!!
text: Just got it today and I listened to Miss Franklin and some classical instrumentals; oh my heavens!! The sound is of supreme quality (will wait for jazz later and I know I'll be mesmerized). Very comfortable to wear and I will have peace when I walk my dogs with these on. Best investment I've! Get it and you won't regret. And to naysayers re case, why would one carry it for 4-6hrs while listening? I don't see the logic of bulky complaints. Charge them before you go somewhere

title: What AirPods should be...
text:
Wouldn't you know it, I bought these for me and the wife a month before Apple came out with the AirPods Pro with noise canceling. Noise canceling is one of those things you think you don't need until you try it. The only people who don't need noise cancelation are people who listen to music in a totally quiet environment.

But these don't claim to be noise canceling, so they absolutely do what they are supposed to do, and do it beautifully. I love these earphones, and you will too. Until you try ones with noise cancelation....D'OH!

title: Won't charge after less than a year
text: I bought these last March 2021, and now, Feb 2022 right before one year mark, the charger won't charge! I have tried everything I found on the internet to reset, restart, etc... but looks like I have to buy a charging case which is $99! Don't waste your money on this! Even before this happened there were times when it only charged one headphone, or one headphone wouldn't connect to my iPhone! I believe they have a hardware design flaw from everything I've read from other users.

title: Beats for life!
text: Super impressed!!! Very nice, charging is awesome, love these. Had my last pair for 3+ years, so happy to finally upgrade

title: Great workout headphones
text: Perfect for working out. They stay put with the over the ear piece. Great alternative for those who do not like how Apple AirPods feel.

title: Strong battery life but
text:
They have a weird fit, they definitely don’t cancel noise at all. My ears are very small though, like I can’t wear normal earplugs because they don’t fit.
Sound quality is a 7/10
However, the battery life is amazing.. I get about 10 hours of battery usage.
They will make your ears a little sore because of how hard the plug part is but you can avoid that by taking breaks regularly.
They kind of look like hearing aids as well, so that’s a negative for me.
Overall, I wouldn’t pay $200 for them again.. maybe $120.

title: Love!!
text: I was skeptical about these because I haven’t had any luck with wireless headphones but these are AMAZING! I’ve used apple AirPods for as long as I can remember and these are so much better! Easy to setup, put on, stays on and sounds great! Love!

title: Charging issues
text:
Great sound quality and just a great set of headphones....until they started to have issues charge about 4 months in. What started as just the left headphone has now turned into both headphones never charging correctly, even if placed correctly in the case. After a year I can now see spots of corrosion on the charging prongs.

Overpriced for the short life span these will give you. Buy some Raycons instead.

title: Worth every penny
text: These are amazing and so comfortable I will be buying more for my family!

title: Comfortable and sound good!
text: At last, I finally found a pair of over ear buds that are comfortable and sound good. The case magnet is a little difficult to open, but they are easy to pair with my Samsung Note 20 Ultra. I downloaded the beats app and a bass boost app. They work well together.

title: Great product and quick shipment!  Exactly as advertised.
text: Great product and quick shipment!  Exactly as advertised.

title: 2nd Beats Earbuds does this to me
text: Right side earphone does not pair. After a few times of usage. This is really frustrating

title: Great sounds with EQ app
text:
Tried a friend's PBPs before purchasing and was verrrry hesitant. I am not a fan of unbalanced music and it seemed like these produced far too much bass for my liking. An EQ app to reduce the low range, boost the mid ranges and generally balance the sound out was just the solution. These are now my very favorite headphones.

9/24/2020
Changing rating from 5 to 3 stars. These sporadically disconnect from my phone at least once/day. I have to turn off blue tooth, then re-pair. Sound is still good.

title: Beats Pro
text: Beats are already a pretty good brand- you get a lot of “boom” for your buck. I also love the different color options that were available through Amazon! Great family gifts of various colors.

title: Son loves the headphones
text: Gift for our 16 year old. He loves them.

title: Fantastic Water Use and Sound Quality
text:
Why I Purchased Them: To use while bouncing around in the pool, traveling and when working in the yard. I tested these today while in the pool (but not under water) and they were FANTASTIC. The sound quality is incredible.

Likes:
1. How secure they fill in my ears.
2. Comfort
3. Sound Quality
3+ - The Color Options

Dislikes:
1. The directions weren't real clear on how to proceed to the next song, without finishing the one that you were currently listening to. Figured out by trial and error (2 quick taps on the main button).
2. Wish the charging cord fit into the charging case for easier traveling.

Would I Purchase Again:
Most definitely YES! This has been a great addition to my tech tools.

title: From great to JUNK in 60 days.
text:
Okay last review. Do not buy. They only work about 1 in 5 attempts to use them. My Bose work every time. My cheaper gear works every time. These are way to crappy for the money.

Adding to original review. The right side still does not work on occasion so you really have to baby these things. Not happy to have to do that.

First problem was the right side had no sound. A reset fixed that. Now the right side is not charging and is discharging faster than the left side. I think there must be something I'm doing but haven't figured out what yet.

title: Never spent this much on headphones
text: After going through multiple headphones for under $50. I decided to pull the trigger on these because I’m training for a marathon. It pairs well with the garmin 945. Best pair for running hands down.

title: Good sound
text: I like these beats. The only downfall for me is when I'm listening to music from my phone I have to change the song on my phone. There's no rewind or fast foward button on them. Also until I got use to them I accidentally hung up on a lot of people. When the battery dies on them you just put them back in the case for a little bit and they charge right in the case.

title: Note these are headphones ONLY
text: Please note these are headphones only. You will need airpods for calls. But they stay on your ears, & sound great.

title: case issues
text: The only problem i have is that it is so smooth and slick that i had to put rubber pads to just open the thing. and so it will not slide away.

title: Best of the best
text: These are by far the best ear buds I have tried. Easy to set up and the quality is awesome. So much better than my air pods. Highly recommend.

title: bass is non existent
text: Ok, based on what I have read about Beats in general these are a massive disappointment. I've only had them for one day but based on the price and reputation if you are looking for good bass you're going to be disappointed. These have a lot of the things you want out of an apple product, instant reliable connection to Apple products, a nice look, good battery (from what I read) but the sound quality is very meh. Now, I will say one thing, Apple locks up EQ for its on music only, which could make a huge difference, but really I'm not impressed. The sound clarity is pretty good, but if you listen to anything other than Apple Music you're going to be disappointed. Additionally, iPhone only allows you to change listening environments not a true equalizer. Anyway, I would look at some of the other brands, even Monster the original designer, might be a better bet at less than 1/2 the price.

title: Life Changing
text:
These headphones are INCREDIBLE. You can control the volume, pause, skip, and repeat songs ON both earbuds. They have a great range and battery life, too. I can't stand AirPods because they never fit comfortably for all-day wear.

These are perfect. Figuring out how to put them in my ears took some getting used to, but the three sizes of ear plugs (provided in the box) are helpful in getting a good fit. These are not noise-canceling. Beats says they have "noise isolation," but it is still difficult to hear what's happening around you when you use both earbuds.

I bought the red ones (not sure why they have a price markup). They are orangish-red in person, so if you are looking for bright red, you'll be disappointed. My favorite thing about them was pairing them with my iPhone, iPad, and Mac. The headphones communicate with the device and basically set themselves up. Super easy.

They sound great, too. In typical Beats fashion, the bass is on point. Aft

title: Returning these
text: I selected these after all the raving reviews here and elsewhere. Giving it two stars, as the pairing to my iphone was effortless and it looks well constructed. But I cannot get a comfortable fit. Only the largest silicone ear piece fits in my ear, but barely. I don't have large ears but I think my ear canal is kind of deep so I can't get the ear piece to make a tight seal to get the best sound. Also, because I have to push these in so far to get some sound, they hurt my outer ear and I can't wear them for more than an hour.  I've tried them for three days and I'm done.

title: Great for Workouts
text: I use these headphones for listening to music while working out. They work great! I love not having a wire between them. They do use battery differently between the two, which is strangee.

title: Perfect if it were cheaper.
text: I only use these for exercise, and I could tell you from the 2 weeks of experience I've had them for that they won't fall off no matter how sweaty or how much movement you produce. The sound is obviously better than most of the "sports" earbuds out there but the main focus here is mobility and stability and with the ear hooks and true wireless aspect they made a winner here; the only problem I have with these are the price if the price were around 180 instead they'd be perfect. Regardless of that fact I still love these fantastic product.

title: Best Earbuds yet!
text: We purchased these on the recommendation of a co-worker for our son. He previously had the Samsung Buds Live and we had to send them in twice to be repaired. He loves to work out and play video games and these are perfect for both! While I did tell him not to shower with them, he keeps saying he will. I am quite sure he already has. He mentioned the sound was much clearer compared to the Samsung. They are also more comfortable than the Samsung. You do have to download an app to us it with android. It was not exceedingly difficult to do. After that it is good to go. I will definitely be buying a pair for myself when the times comes.

title: Earphones
text: Good sound, stsybin your ear.  Easy to psir with app or without

title: Great Wireless Headphones
text:
Great sound, great battery life. I’ve used them outside and people say they can hear me very clear.

Integration with the iPhone is wonderful. I like the power display on the iPhone or iPad. It shows power remaining on each ear piece and on the charging base, all in one display. Its also very easy to switch from iPhone use to iPad when you need to.

They are very comfortable and come with different sized ear pieces. The behind the ear design of the holder, keeps the headset in no matter what you do.

title: Great sound, great headphones — will NOT fall out!
text:
Great for working out and everyday use. Great sound. I have yet to experience problems with battery life. I'm not even consistent about charging it and it hasn't died on me. I've found that even charging the case in the car (because it has a lighting USB) is enough.

These are not noise cancelling so if that's a deal breaker maybe try airpods pro. Still easily can enjoy music or Podcasts in noisy environments.

Beats can sometimes be over rated and maybe they're a tad too expensive for the product but not enough to change my mind.

title: Powerful reception
text: I had a black Powerbeats pro myself, I bought a white one for my wife. She is using it all day every day now. She keeps her phone on main floor and can go upstairs of downstairs, her reception is not interrupted. Quality of sound is great, reception is over 30 feet, you get what you pay for.

title: Prowerbeats Pro
text: I guess there is always going to be some negative reviews? But! I very happy with the Powerbeats Pro! Yes they are a little expensive, and some think they are too big! But they are what I need! Don’t fall out! Fit comfortably! Look great!! I let you know how they last! They sound just as good as any Bluetooth I’ve own! Easy to operate! Check YouTube for tips operating them!

title: Lasted less than 2 years - Maybe expecting too much!
text: Bought powerbeat pro earbuds in May 2020. Maybe I am expecting too much for the $199 earbuds, but one of them now only lasts about 1 -2 hours and the other 5 hours max. It takes longer than 5 minutes to recharge, in fact considerably longer! Usually, they are pretty comfortable and I can forget I have the hook wrapped around my ear, but some days it gets to be too much. I did buy the extended warranty, but unfortunately I apparently destroyed the box with the serial number for the item and Asurion nor Amazon is able to help with replacement. A refurbished pair only have a 90-day warranty. I am not going back with this brand.

title: These have good sound, but the devil is in pairing and constant one ear only working
text: Product has good battery life and sound.  However it’s all ruined by the constant pairing trying to get one or the other to work and then making sure it’s charging in the case which often leaves one dead if you don’t check.  I find myself dealing with two issues daily.  Ruins the entire experience.  Had to issue a one star because these shouldn’t happen at this price point

title: Love them
text: I really like the Powerbeats Pro.  I had the Jabra Elite Active 65t earbuds but they always fell out when I was working out.  They are great if not in a sweaty environment.  The Powerbeats Pro are perfect for the gym.  No matter what I am doing, they do not fall out.  The sound quality is great.  Highly recommended.

title: Failed after six months
text: Odd problem, but the right earbud just no longer wants to charge. Otherwise, great product, but having it fail in six months just isn't acceptable.

title: Great earphones!
text: I’m so happy I sprung and bought these. They connect as soon as I open the case so by the time I get my shoes on at the gym, I’m already jamming out. I don’t hear any background noise at the gym. - only my music. Big thumbs up!

title: Worked for two months
text: Junk ! Defective! Something - worked for two months. Too much treble

title: Great product!
text: Best wireless I've used. Kind of weird that the box is needed to paid the buds, but oh well.

title: Amazing
text: The best Beats I have ever purchased. It’s going to be difficult for the company to top this. Everything from the sound to the fit is amazing.

title: Didn’t work, sent for repairs, doesn’t work again
text: Been for repairs. Not working again now. First time wouldn’t ‘talk’ to devices. Then only 1 would work. Now other earpiece won’t charge. Apple store sent for repairs, takes two weeks, then two weeks later doesn’t work again. Not a good experience, especially given the price. Husband has a pair too and those have issues too. Two pair, two duds. 0 for 2.

title: Sound quality
text: The sound and noise quality is excellent.  Love it!

title: Uncomfortable and surprisingly low volume
text:
Plastic puts pressure on the rear of your inner ear so that it becomes uncomfortable. You have to turn them 3/4 to full volume to hear much of anything.
Surprising miss on Beats' part. Maybe V2 will be better.

title: Junk
text: These are terrible. You have to forget device and reconnect every use or it will only play out of one bud and it’s a act of congress to get fixed. Less than a 6 months old.

title: Very worthy!
text:
Love working out in these! Don’t fall out. Great sound, not true noise cancellation but not concerned about that as I like to have some awareness around me.
Just wish the case was a bit more compact although not sure if that’s possible considering the size of the ear buds.

title: Worth every penny
text: I tried to be cheap and avoid these as workout buds, but in the end you get what you pay for. I knew I would love these for the sole purpose of the "hooks" that go around your ear. Making these the best workout earbuds. Completely wireless and sweat resistant. The battery is a beast, especially when you factor in the huge case that brings another 20 hours of charge to the buds. The sound quality is excellent and the noise cancelation is driven by a tight seal in the ear canal. I bought these "like new" for a $40 discount. They came packaged as normal without the seal. This is the best route in my opinion!
